messages.content:
What minimum information should you include in your reference list about a contribution to a collective or editorial work? 

 

		
Author(s) of the contribution. (year of publication). Title of the contribution. In name of the editor(s) (Eds.), Name of the collective work. Publisher

		
Author(s) of the contribution. (year of publication). Title of the contribution (page numbers). Publisher

		
Author(s) of the contribution. (year of publication). Title of contribution (page numbers). Title of entire work. DOI number

		
Author(s) of the contribution. (year of publication). Title of the contribution. In name of the editor(s) (Eds.), Title of the collective work (page numbers). Publisher

The correct answer is:

Author(s) of the contribution. (year of publication). Title of the contribution. In name of the editor(s) (Eds.), Title of the collective work (page numbers). Publisher

Which way of referring is correct:


Extensive research has already been carried out into the impact of business characteristics on Audit Report Lag (ARL) on the one hand (Givoly & Palmon, 1982; Ashton,  Graul & Newton, 1989; Carslaw & Kaplan, 1991; Bamber et al. 1993; Al-Ajmi, 2008; Afify, 2009; Habib & Bhuiyan, 2011; Knechel & Sharma, 2012; Ashtana, 2014b; Dao & Pham, 2014a), and …
Extensive research has already been carried out into the impact of business characteristics on Audit Report Lag (ARL) on the one hand (Afify, 2009; Al-Ajmi, 2008; Ashtana, 2014b; Ashton et al. 1989; Bamber et al. 1993; Carslaw & Kaplan, 1991; Dao & Pham, 2014b; Givoly & Palmon, 1982; Habib & Bhuiyan, 2011; Knechel & Sharma, 2012), and …
Extensive research has already been carried out into the impact of business characteristics on Audit Report Lag (ARL) on the one hand (Afify, 2009; Al-Ajmi, 2008; Ashton, Graul & Newton, 1989; Ashtana, 2014b; Bamber et al. 1993; Carslaw & Kaplan, 1991; Dao & Pham, 2014b; Givoly & Palmon, 1982; Habib & Bhuiyan, 2011; Knechel & Sharma, 2012), and …
Extensive research has already been carried out into the impact of business characteristics on Audit Report Lag (ARL) on the one hand (Givoly & Palmon, 1982; Ashton, Graul & Newton, 1989; Carslaw & Kaplan, 1991; Bamber et al. 1993; Al-Ajmi, 2008; Afify, 2009; Habib & Bhuiyan, 2011; Knechel & Sharma, 2012; Dao & Pham, 2014a; Ashtana, 2014b), and …

The correct way of referring is:

Extensive research has already been carried out into the impact of business characteristics on Audit Report Lag (ARL) on the one hand (Afify, 2009; Al-Ajmi, 2008; Ashton et al., 1989; Ashtana, 2014b; Bamber et al., 1993; Carslaw & Kaplan, 1991; Dao & Pham, 2014b; Givoly & Palmon, 1982; Habib & Bhuiyan, 2011; Knechel & Sharma, 2012), and …

Proper paraphrasing is ...

 

		
writing someone else's text in your own words and mentioning the source.

		
changing a few words in someone else's text, so that the text becomes more 'yours' and mentioning the source.

		
using an idea from someone else's text, in a completely different way and not mentioning the source.

		
putting someone else's text in quotation marks and mentioning the source.
Proper paraphrasing is: writing someone else's text in your own words and mentioning the source.

In a text by a student it says the following:

Burnout is a syndrome of extreme fatigue (exhaustion), distance from work (distance) and little confidence in one's own ability (reduced competence), the causes of which lie either in the working atmosphere or in the way in which employees deal with stressful aspects in their work.  (Taris et al., 2013).

The original text is:

Burnout is a syndrome of extreme fatigue (exhaustion), distance from work (distance) and little confidence in one's own ability (reduced competence), the causes of which lie either in the working atmosphere or in the way in which employees deal with stressful aspects in their work. 

The reference data of the source is: Taris, T., Houtman, I., & Schaufeli, W. (2013). Burnout: de stand van zaken. Tijdschrift voor Arbeidsvraagstukken, 29(3), 241-257. Consulted on 15 april 2019 via https://www.wilmarschaufeli.nl/publications/Schaufeli/410.pdf

Which improvement is correct?


		
Burnout is a syndrome of extreme fatigue (exhaustion), distancing oneself from one's work (distance) and little confidence in one's own (reduced competence), the causes of which lie either in the working atmosphere or in the way in which employees deal with stressful aspects in their work (Taris, Houtman & Schaufeli, 2013).

		
Taris, Houtman and Schaufeli, 2013, p. 241: "Burnout is a syndrome of extreme fatigue (exhaustion), distancing oneself from work (distance) and little confidence in one's own ability (reduced competence), the causes of which lie either in the working atmosphere or in the way in which employees deal with stressful aspects in their work". 

		
According to Taris et al. (2013, p. 241 ) "burnout is [...] a syndrome of extreme fatigue (exhaustion), distance from work (distance) and little confidence in one's own ability (reduced competence), the causes of which lie either in the working atmosphere or in the way in which employees deal with stressful aspects in their work.” 

		
"Burnout is a syndrome of extreme fatigue, distancing oneself from work and a lack of confidence in one's own ability, the causes of which lie either in the working atmosphere or in the way in which employees deal with stressful aspects of their work" (Taris, Houtman & Schaufeli, 2013).

The correct improvement is:

According to Taris et al. (2013, p. 241 ) "burnout is [...] a syndrome of extreme fatigue (exhaustion), distance from work (distance) and little confidence in one's own ability (reduced competence), the causes of which lie either in the working atmosphere or in the way in which employees deal with stressful aspects in their work.”

A text contains the following sentence:

The strong rise of shale gas and oil in the US creates the biggest threat to OPEC's market position (Koster, n.d.; Marquez, 2018; Vranckx & Ophoff, 2014).

 

		
The order of the references is correct.

		
The correct order of the references is: Vranckx & Ophoff, 2014; Marquez, 2018; Koster, n.d.

		
The correct order of the references is: Koster, n.d.; Vranckx & Ophoff, 2014; Marquez, 2018.

		
The correct order of references is: Marquez, 2018; Vranckx & Ophoff, 2014; Koster, n.d.
The correct order of the references is: Vranckx & Ophoff, 2014; Marquez, 2018; Koster, n.d.

Information from a video may be used as a source in a paper provided you refer to it correctly.

Which of the following statements is correct?


		
Yes, but only if the video comes from a site like vrt.be or standard.be.

		
Yes, if you refer to it correctly and the information from that video indicates the relevance of your subject.

		
No, if the video is from YouTube.

		
No, because you can never refer to a video.
Yes, if you refer to it correctly and the information from that video indicates the relevance of your subject.

To base your entire paper on the ideas and findings of one article, in which you do refer to this article, is considered to be ...


		
an acceptable working method if you did not find another article on the subject via the search functions of the University Library website.



		
no plagiarism, but it is an unacceptable way of working.



		
plagiarism.



		
an acceptable method of working.
an acceptable method of working.

Someone who participates in your assignment has taken a picture that can be used in your project. You want to use that photo and include it in your paper. When you alter this photo with a graphic program, you do not have to ask for the photographer's permission.

Is this statement correct?

 

		
By editing the photo, you become the 'author' of it and you can freely use the image.



		
If the photographer delivers the image to you, he or she waives his or her intellectual property rights and you are free to edit and publish this image.



		
The image is the intellectual property of the photographer. You must ask permission to edit this image and even after editing it, you have to include a reference to the original photographer in the paper.



		
You have to ask the photographer for permission to edit the image. After all, it is his/her intellectual property. However, as soon as you have that permission and edit the image, it becomes your image and you do not have to make a reference to the original photographer.

The correct statement is:

The image is the intellectual property of the photographer. You must ask permission to edit this image and even after editing it, you have to include a reference to the original photographer in the paper.

What is not a decisive reason to use references?


		
You have to use references because in that way you prove you have mastered the reference style.



		
You have use references because this is how you show whether or not you master the subject.



		
You have use references to prove your intellectual honesty.



		
You must use references in order for your text to be verifiable by third parties.
The statement "You have to use references because in that way you prove you have mastered the reference style" is not a decisive reason to use references.

For which of the examples do you not need to mention the source?


		
When I paraphrase in my own words an idea from another author.



		
When I have found data from a published survey and pour it into a table.



		
When I use information from the slides of lessons.



		
When I formulate my own idea.
The example where you formulate your own idea does not require mentioning the source.
